optOut.comment: Banner phase: pick exactly one action to dismiss the banner (or open the preferences modal). Wrapped in a nested if/else chain so a successful direct-reject click does NOT also run the tertiary / banner-description branches — on layouts like PBS where #ketch-banner-button-tertiary is 'Cookie Settings' that would re-open preferences after opt-out.

then.comment: Prefer a direct reject-style button in the banner or preferences modal (any Ketch DOM variant). Ketch's primary/secondary/tertiary ids reflect visual hierarchy, not role, so match on aria-label instead: covers 'Reject All', 'Reject Optional Cookies', etc.

Some Ketch variants show the preferences modal directly (no banner phase) and treat Reject All as 'toggle all off' — the choice is not saved until Confirm/Save is clicked. If the modal is still visible, submit it. Selectors mirror the modal-handling step below.

then.click: #lanyard_root button[class*=confirmButton], #lanyard_root div[class*=actions_] > button:nth-child(1), #lanyard_root button[class*=actionButton], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Confirm'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Confirm'], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Save Settings'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Save Settings']

else.comment: Legacy fallback: some Ketch banners expose the direct reject button as #ketch-banner-button-tertiary without an aria-label prefix match.
Older banner variant exposed via aria-describedby=banner-description: open settings/reject (same button class). The preferences modal is handled by the trailing step below.

then.comment: can be either settings or reject button

Disclosure-only banner (e.g. US/CCPA notice with a single 'I understand'/'OK' acknowledge button and no reject option). Clicking the lone button dismisses the notice; consent state is already determined by Ketch's jurisdiction defaults.

optOut.comment: Preferences/purposes modal is open (typically after opening settings from the banner): prefer Reject All then Confirm; otherwise (e.g. US/CCPA variant with only per-purpose toggles) uncheck every enabled switch and save. Skipped if the banner phase already dismissed everything.

if.exists: #lanyard_root button[class*=rejectButton], #lanyard_root button[class*=rejectAllButton], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Reject All'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Reject All']

then.waitForThenClick: #lanyard_root button[class*=rejectButton], #lanyard_root button[class*=rejectAllButton], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Reject All'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Reject All']

else.comment: Fallback for jurisdictions where the modal has no 'Reject All': toggle off each currently-enabled purpose switch.

then.click: #lanyard_root button[class*=confirmButton], #lanyard_root div[class*=actions_] > button:nth-child(1), #lanyard_root button[class*=actionButton], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Confirm'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Confirm'], #ketch-modal button[aria-label='Save Settings'], #ketch-purposes-modal button[aria-label='Save Settings']
